description: Next.js 16 project initialized with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and all core dependencies installed
steps: Run create-next-app with TypeScript and App Router
Install dependencies: @auth0/nextjs-auth0, resend, @react-email/components, zod
Install UI dependencies: tailwindcss, shadcn/ui
Create tsconfig.json with strict mode
Create .env.example with all required variables
Create .gitignore
Verify npm run dev starts without errors on port 3001
Verify npm run build succeeds

description: Environment config validation — app fails fast on startup if any required env var is missing
steps: Create lib/config.ts with Zod schema for all env vars
Validate AUTH0_DOMAIN, AUTH0_M2M_CLIENT_ID, AUTH0_M2M_CLIENT_SECRET, AUTH0_DB_CONNECTION
Validate AUTH0_CLIENT_ID, AUTH0_CLIENT_SECRET, AUTH0_SECRET, APP_BASE_URL
Validate CIRCLE_API_TOKEN, CIRCLE_COMMUNITY_ID
Validate RESEND_API_KEY, EMAIL_FROM
Validate PASSWORD_TICKET_TTL, PASSWORD_TICKET_RESULT_URL
App throws clear error on missing var
Write unit test for config validation

description: TypeScript interfaces defined for all domain types
steps: Create types/index.ts
Define CircleMember interface (id, email, name, avatar_url, access_groups, etc.)
Define CircleAccessGroup interface (id, name, description)
Define ProvisioningStatus type (not_provisioned, auth0_created, email_sent, failed)
Define MemberWithStatus interface (CircleMember + auth0Status + emailStatus)
Define CreateMemberPayload interface
Define MigratePayload interface
Define Auth0User interface
Verify types compile without errors

description: Auth0 v4 middleware protects /dashboard/* routes and auth handler is configured
steps: Create proxy.ts at project root with Next.js 16 proxy pattern (replaces deprecated middleware.ts)
Configure route matcher for /dashboard/:path*
Create app/api/auth/[auth0]/route.ts for login/logout/callback
Verify unauthenticated access to /dashboard redirects to Auth0 login
Verify authenticated admin can access /dashboard
Write test for middleware route protection

description: Utility helpers — random password generator and app/page.tsx redirect to /dashboard
steps: Create lib/utils.ts generateRandomPassword() using crypto.randomBytes (32 chars, mixed case, numbers, symbols)
Meets Auth0 default password policy (min 8 chars, uppercase, lowercase, number, special)
Update app/page.tsx to redirect to /dashboard using next/navigation redirect()
Write unit test for generateRandomPassword — length, character classes
Verify redirect works: visiting / goes to /dashboard

description: Auth0 Management API client — M2M token fetching with cache and user CRUD operations
steps: Create lib/auth0-management.ts
Implement getManagementToken() with cache based on expires_in minus 5min safety margin
Implement getUserByEmail(email) — GET /api/v2/users-by-email
Implement createUser(email, name, metadata) — POST /api/v2/users with random password
Implement createPasswordTicket(userId, resultUrl, ttl) — POST /api/v2/tickets/password-change
Implement updateUserMetadata(userId, metadata) — PATCH /api/v2/users/{id}
Random password: 32 chars via crypto.randomBytes, meets Auth0 policy
Handle 409 Conflict (user already exists) gracefully
Write unit tests with mocked HTTP calls

description: Circle.so Admin API v2 client — list members, create members, manage access groups
steps: Create lib/circle-api.ts
Implement listMembers(communityId) — GET /community_members with pagination handling
Implement createMember(communityId, email, name) — POST /community_members
Implement listAccessGroups(communityId) — GET /access_groups
Implement addMemberToGroup(groupId, email) — POST /access_groups/{id}/members
Handle pagination: per_page=100, check has_next_page, loop if needed
Auth header: Authorization: Bearer <CIRCLE_API_TOKEN>
Base URL: https://app.circle.so/api/admin/v2
Write unit tests with mocked HTTP calls

description: Resend email client with React Email welcome template
steps: Create emails/welcome-email.tsx with React Email components
Branded template: HelpUcompli logo area, welcome message, 'Set Your Password' button
Dynamic fields: {name}, {passwordTicketUrl}, {expiryDays}
Subject: 'Set up your HelpUcompli account'
Create lib/resend-email.ts
Implement sendWelcomeEmail(to, name, passwordTicketUrl)
From address: EMAIL_FROM env var
Handle Resend API errors gracefully
Write unit test for email rendering and sending

description: GET /api/circle/members — returns Circle.so community members with Auth0 provisioning status
steps: Create app/api/circle/members/route.ts
Validate Auth0 session before processing
Fetch all Circle.so members via circle-api.ts
For each member, check Auth0 by email to determine provisioning status
Read app_metadata.email_sent to determine email status
Return JSON array of MemberWithStatus objects
Handle Circle API errors with proper error responses
Write integration test

description: GET /api/circle/access-groups — returns Circle.so access groups
steps: Create app/api/circle/access-groups/route.ts
Validate Auth0 session before processing
Fetch access groups via circle-api.ts
Return JSON array of CircleAccessGroup objects
Write integration test

description: POST /api/provision/migrate — migrates existing Circle.so member to Auth0
steps: Create app/api/provision/migrate/route.ts
Validate Auth0 session before processing
Parse and validate request body: { email, name, circleMemberId }
Check if user already exists in Auth0 (skip if yes, return 'already_provisioned')
Create Auth0 user with random password and app_metadata.source='admin_provisioning'
Generate password-change ticket (7-day TTL, mark_email_as_verified: true)
Send welcome email via Resend with ticket URL
Update Auth0 app_metadata: email_sent=true, email_sent_at=ISO timestamp
Return success response with provisioning details
Handle partial failures: if email fails after Auth0 creation, return status indicating retry needed
Write integration test

description: POST /api/provision/create — creates new member in both Circle.so and Auth0
steps: Create app/api/provision/create/route.ts
Validate Auth0 session before processing
Parse and validate request body: { firstName, lastName, email, accessGroupId }
Step 1: Create member in Circle.so via circle-api.ts
Step 2: Add member to access group via circle-api.ts
Step 3: Create Auth0 user with random password
Step 4: Generate password-change ticket
Step 5: Send welcome email via Resend
Step 6: Update Auth0 app_metadata with email_sent tracking
Handle partial failures at each step with appropriate error messages
If Circle succeeds but Auth0 fails, return error indicating Circle member created
Write integration test

description: GET /api/status — returns provisioning status for all Circle members by checking Auth0 app_metadata
steps: Create app/api/status/route.ts (replace stub)
Validate Auth0 session before processing
Fetch all Circle.so members via circle-api.ts
For each member, check Auth0 by email via getUserByEmail()
Derive status from app_metadata: not_provisioned / auth0_created / email_sent
Return JSON array of MemberWithStatus objects
Handle case where Auth0 returns no user (not provisioned)
Handle case where app_metadata.email_sent is missing (auth0_created)
Write integration test

description: Dashboard layout with navigation — auth-protected with Auth0 org admin check
steps: Create app/layout.tsx with Auth0 UserProvider
Create app/dashboard/layout.tsx with sidebar navigation
Navigation tabs: 'Existing Members' and 'Add New Member'
Show logged-in admin name and logout button
Responsive layout with Tailwind CSS
Verify only authenticated org admins can access
Test layout renders correctly

description: Toast notifications (Sonner) and loading states integrated into dashboard layout
steps: Add Sonner Toaster component to app/layout.tsx or dashboard/layout.tsx
Create reusable loading skeleton for member table (components/member-table-skeleton.tsx)
Show skeleton while /api/circle/members is loading
Show error toast on any API failure with descriptive message
Show success toast on successful provisioning
Test toast appears on API error simulation

description: Existing Members tab — table showing Circle.so members with Auth0 status and migrate buttons
steps: Create app/dashboard/page.tsx
Create components/member-table.tsx
Create components/status-badge.tsx
Create components/provision-button.tsx
Fetch members from GET /api/circle/members on page load
Display table: Name | Email | Circle Access Groups | Auth0 Status | Action
Status badges: 'Not Provisioned' (red), 'Provisioned' (green), 'Email Sent' (blue)
'Migrate' button per row calls POST /api/provision/migrate
Loading spinner per row during provisioning
Update status badge on completion
Error toast on failure
Test table renders with mock data

description: Migrate All button — bulk migration with sequential processing and progress indicator
steps: Add 'Migrate All Unprovisioned' button above the member table
On click: process unprovisioned members sequentially with 200ms delay
Show progress indicator: 'Migrating 5/30...'
Disable button during processing to prevent double-clicks
Update each row's status badge as it completes
Show summary toast on completion: 'X migrated, Y failed'
Handle rate limit errors with retry logic
Test bulk migration flow

description: Add New Member form — creates member in Circle.so + Auth0 with access group
steps: Create app/dashboard/new-member/page.tsx
Create components/new-member-form.tsx
Form fields: First Name, Last Name, Email, Access Group (dropdown)
Access group dropdown populated from GET /api/circle/access-groups
Client-side validation: email format, required fields (Zod)
On submit: POST /api/provision/create
Success toast: 'Member created and welcome email sent'
Error toast with specific failure details
Server-side duplicate email check
Reset form on success
Test form validation and submission

description: Retry Email button for users where Auth0 was created but email failed
steps: Show 'Retry Email' button for members with status 'auth0_created' (yellow badge)
On click: regenerate password ticket + resend email
Update app_metadata on success
Update status badge to 'Email Sent' (blue/green)
Test retry flow

description: End-to-end flow — migrate one existing member from Circle.so to Auth0 and verify email
steps: Start dev server with valid env vars
Log in as admin via Auth0
Navigate to Existing Members tab
Verify Circle.so members are displayed
Click 'Migrate' on one member
Verify Auth0 account created (check Auth0 Dashboard)
Verify welcome email received (check Resend dashboard or inbox)
Verify password-set link works and redirects to compass.helpucompli.com
Verify member shows as 'Provisioned + Email Sent' in dashboard

description: End-to-end flow — add one new member to Circle.so and Auth0
steps: Navigate to Add New Member tab
Fill in first name, last name, email, select access group
Click 'Create'
Verify member created in Circle.so (check Circle admin panel)
Verify member assigned to correct access group
Verify Auth0 account created
Verify welcome email received
Verify member appears in Existing Members tab with 'Provisioned' status

description: Duplicate detection — migrating an already-provisioned member shows correct status
steps: Attempt to migrate a member who already has an Auth0 account
Verify system returns 'already_provisioned' response
Verify no duplicate Auth0 account created
Verify status badge shows 'Already Provisioned'
Test with both UI button click and direct API call

description: Partial failure recovery — Circle.so created but Auth0 fails, email retry works
steps: Simulate: Add new member where Circle.so succeeds but Auth0 creation fails
Verify error message indicates 'Created in Circle but Auth0 failed'
Verify the member appears in Existing Members tab (pulled from Circle.so)
Verify admin can retry migration for that member from Existing Members tab
Simulate: Auth0 created but Resend email fails
Verify 'Retry Email' button appears (yellow status badge)
Click 'Retry Email' — verify email sends and status updates to green
Verify app_metadata.email_sent updates correctly after retry

description: Email status tracking persists across page refreshes via Auth0 app_metadata
steps: Migrate a member — verify status shows 'Email Sent' (green)
Refresh the page — verify status still shows 'Email Sent' (not reset)
Open in a new browser tab — verify same status
Check Auth0 Dashboard — confirm app_metadata has email_sent=true and email_sent_at
Verify status derivation: no Auth0 user = red, Auth0 user without email_sent = yellow, with email_sent = green

description: All API routes validate Auth0 session and admin role before processing
steps: Every POST /api/provision/* route checks Auth0 session
Every GET /api/circle/* route checks Auth0 session
Unauthenticated requests return 401
Non-admin authenticated users return 403
Test with missing session, expired session, and non-admin user
